title: rfo-23: Rollback support
description: Implement rfo rollback (run-id): reset repo to pre-run OID when safe, delete created branch when safe, show manual recovery steps when automatic rollback is unsafe. Rollback creates a new run record. No fake guarantees.

title: rfo-3: Repo spec parser
description: Implement the GitHub repo spec parser that accepts formats: owner/repo, github.com/owner/repo, https://github.com/owner/repo, etc. Output a RepoSpec struct with host, owner, name, branch, alias, clone_url. Reject non-GitHub formats. This is pure logic in rfo-core.
close_reason: RepoSpec::parse() implemented with 17 tests. Supports owner/repo, github.com/owner/repo, https, git@, #branch, as alias. Rejects non-github schemes.

title: rfo-0: Workspace bootstrap
description: Bootstrap the workspace: create top-level Cargo.toml as workspace root, rust-toolchain.toml with Rust 2024 edition and MSRV 1.85+, .cargo/config.toml for build config, deny.toml for cargo-deny policy. Set up crate directory structure for crates/rfo, rfo-core, rfo-config, rfo-output, rfo-state, rfo-git, rfo-github, rfo-jobs, rfo-sync, rfo-context, rfo-review, rfo-sweep, rfo-provider, rfo-mcp, rfo-testkit. No code yet — just workspace plumbing. This unblocks all crate-level work.
close_reason: Workspace bootstrap complete: 16 crates, toolchain, deny.toml, config, .gitignore. cargo check --workspace passes clean.

title: rfo-11: GitHub repo lookup
description: Implement rfo-github repo lookup via octocrab: fetch repo metadata (owner, name, default_branch, visibility, archived). Used by rfo add to validate and enrich repo specs. Cache results in SQLite.

title: Phase 0: Workspace bootstrap
description: Bootstrap the workspace: initialize Cargo workspace, set up CI, create rfo-core, rfo-config, rfo-output, and SQLite migrations

title: rfo-47: Doctor
description: Implement rfo doctor: verify installation, git availability, GitHub auth, config validity, SQLite state, provider availability. Optionally repair issues. Idempotent and reversible. Backup-aware.

title: rfo-7: gix read queries
description: Implement rfo-git read-only layer using gix: status(), ahead_behind(), head_oid(), is_dirty(), current_branch(). These are pure queries, no mutations. All functions return typed results, never raw strings.
close_reason: gix read queries: discover, head_oid, current_branch, is_dirty, status, ahead_behind. 14 tests. Hybrid gix+shell-out approach. Closes repo_forge-3e6.

title: rfo-8: Shell-out git wrapper
description: Implement rfo-git mutation layer using std::process::Command: fetch, pull, clone, commit, push, reset_hard. Rules: Command::arg only (no shell interpolation), GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0, LC_ALL=C, --no-pager, capture stdout/stderr, timeout, classify errors.
close_reason: Shell-out git wrapper: fetch, pull, clone, commit, push, reset_hard. Typed opts/outcomes. GitErrorKind classification. 11 tests including end-to-end clone+commit+push. Closes repo_forge-3r9.

title: rfo-21: Failure classification
description: Implement failure memory: classify errors into failure classes (auth_error, rate_limited, merge_conflict, dirty_worktree, network_timeout, missing_git, missing_provider, quality_gate_failed, secret_scan_blocked, github_permission_denied). Store in failures table with fingerprint, count, first/last seen, suggested fix.

title: rfo-24: Risk classification
description: Implement risk classification: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH. Risk reasons: touches many files, modifies CI workflow, modifies dependency file, deletes files, no tests changed, quality gates unavailable, similar failure happened recently. Simple classification, no complex engine.

title: add-4: NDJSON progress
description: Add --output json flag to multi-repo commands (sweep agent, train run) that emits NDJSON event stream: batch_start, repo_start, plan_created, gates_passed, applied, repo_done, gates_failed, batch_done. Human output unchanged when flag omitted. Extends existing NDJSON output mode.

title: rfo-31: Health scoring
description: Implement rfo health [repo]: score 0-100 (90-100 Excellent, 75-89 Healthy, 50-74 Needs attention, 25-49 Risky, 0-24 Critical). Penalties for failed CI, diverged, conflict, dirty worktree, stale PRs, missing files, failed job. Store snapshots in SQLite.

title: rfo-37: Claude provider
description: Implement ClaudeCodeProvider: invoke claude -p --output-format stream-json with configurable args. Parse stream-json output. Handle errors, timeouts, and provider unavailability. Provider is isolated behind a trait.

title: rfo-6: Initial SQLite schema
description: Implement rfo-state crate: open SQLite with WAL mode, foreign keys, busy_timeout. Create migrations for repos, runs, run_events, sync_results, jobs, job_events, plans, failures, repo_health_snapshots, context_cache, audit_log tables. Use sqlx migrate. SQLite is the single source of truth.
close_reason: Full SQLite schema implemented per PLAN.md §13: 12 tables, 8 indexes, PRAGMAs (WAL, foreign_keys, synchronous=NORMAL, busy_timeout=5000); 9 tests pass; clippy/fmt clean

title: add-2: Inbox queue minimal
description: Add rfo inbox next and rfo inbox done commands. inbox next returns JSON with next item (id, repo, priority, reason, suggested_action) or empty object if empty. inbox done marks item as processed (persisted to SQLite). No locking/TTL in v1 (single-agent assumed).

title: rfo-16: Run records
description: Implement run recording: every command that mutates state creates a run record in SQLite (id, command, started_at, ended_at, exit_code, args_json, user, host). Run events are appended for each significant step. Foundation for timeline and audit.

title: rfo-15: Prune
description: Implement rfo prune: remove repos from SQLite that no longer exist on disk or are marked as removed. Interactive confirmation by default, --force to skip. Records audit event.

title: rfo-29: Check status query
description: Implement GitHub check status and CI run lookup via octocrab: fetch check runs, workflow runs, their status and conclusion. Used by inbox, health, and ci autopsy.

title: rfo-19: Run events
description: Implement run event recording: every significant step during a run appends a run_events record (run_id, ts, level, message, data_json). Used by timeline, debugging, and audit. Events are structured, not free-text.

title: rfo-13: Sync engine
description: Implement rfo sync: read repos from SQLite, create run record, for each repo acquire fs4 lock, clone if missing, fetch, calculate ahead/behind, pull per strategy (ff-only default), record pre/post OID, release lock. Parallel execution bounded by config. Emit text/JSON/NDJSON output. Core parity command with ru.

title: rfo-48: Self-update
description: Implement rfo self-update: download latest release from GitHub, verify checksum/signature, replace binary. Must work on Linux (x86_64, aarch64), macOS (x86_64, aarch64), Windows (x86_64).

title: rfo-43: Train plan
description: Implement rfo train plan: discover safe deterministic fixers (cargo fmt, cargo clippy --fix, prettier, ruff --fix, go fmt, npm install --package-lock-only, schema regeneration, lockfile refresh). One small change per PR. Create plan per repo.

title: rfo-14: Status engine
description: Implement rfo status: show repo status (Current, Behind, Ahead, Diverged, Conflict, Dirty, Missing, Unknown) for all or single repo. Use gix for reads. Output in text/JSON/NDJSON/TOON. Must match ru status output format for parity.

title: rfo-39: Quality gates
description: Implement quality gate auto-detection and execution: Rust (cargo fmt --check, cargo test, cargo clippy), Node (npm test, npm run lint, npm run typecheck), Python (pytest, ruff, mypy), Go (go test ./..., go fmt), Shell (shellcheck). Gate results stored as JSON. Gates run before push/apply.

title: rfo-49: cargo-dist release
description: Set up cargo-dist for release builds: linux-x86_64-musl, linux-aarch64-musl, darwin-x86_64, darwin-aarch64, windows-x86_64. Create .github/workflows/release.yml. GitHub Releases as primary distribution.

title: rfo-1: CI fmt/clippy/test/deny/audit
description: Set up GitHub Actions CI: .github/workflows/ci.yml with cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy, cargo test, cargo test --doc. .github/workflows/audit.yml with cargo-deny and cargo-audit. Add deny.toml cargo-deny policy. CI must be green before any PR merges. Also create rust-toolchain.toml if not done in rfo-0.
close_reason: CI workflows (ci.yml + audit.yml) with fmt/clippy/test/deny/audit. All cargo-deny checks pass locally. Pushed and CI triggered.

title: rfo-22: Plan/apply
description: Implement rfo plan and rfo apply: risky operations create a plan (id, kind, repo, summary, operations, risk class, rollback metadata). LOW risk applies normally, MEDIUM requires confirmation, HIGH requires explicit --yes. Every apply creates a run record.

title: rfo-50: Homebrew tap
description: Create Homebrew tap formula for rfo. Auto-update via release workflow. Include install.sh download + verify script.

title: rfo-17: NDJSON compatibility
description: Implement NDJSON output mode for sync and status. Each line is a complete JSON object. Streaming-friendly for piping to other tools. Must match ru NDJSON format for parity.

title: rfo-25: Denylist + secret scan
description: Implement denylist (default: .env, .env.*, *.pem, *.key, id_rsa, id_ed25519, **/.git/**, **/target/**, **/node_modules/**) and secret scan (modes: off/warn/block, default: block). Scan staged files, generated patches, AI edits before commit. Block if secrets detected.

title: add-3: MCP tools
description: Add MCP tools for AI-driven operations: rfo://tools/inbox-next, rfo://tools/inbox-done, rfo://tools/plan-create, rfo://tools/plan-apply (requires confirmed: true), rfo://tools/plan-list, rfo://tools/sweep-agent-plan, rfo://tools/train-run. Strict JSON schema: every mutating tool creates a run record, errors return structured JSON.

title: rfo-35: MCP resources
description: Implement MCP resource endpoints: rfo://repos (list all repos), rfo://inbox (current inbox), rfo://context/{owner}/{repo} (context pack), rfo://runs/{id}/timeline (run timeline). Read-only, structured JSON.

title: rfo-9: fs4 repo lock
description: Implement repo-level file locks using fs4. Before any git mutation, acquire a lock on the repo path. Lock is released after mutation completes or fails. Prevents concurrent rfo processes from corrupting a repo. Lock timeout with clear error message.
close_reason: Implemented RepoLock RAII guard with fs4 (commit c6654c4). 3 lock tests + 25 prior rfo-git tests = 28 pass. Closes rfo-9.

title: rfo-1: CI fmt/clippy/test/deny/audit
description: Set up GitHub Actions CI workflow for the workspace. Required gates: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy, cargo test, cargo-deny, cargo-audit. Also create rust-toolchain.toml pinning Rust 2024 edition and MSRV 1.85+. Include deny.toml for cargo-deny policy. Create .github/workflows/ci.yml, .github/workflows/audit.yml. This is a gate for all subsequent PRs; nothing ships without CI green.

title: rfo-4: XDG config
description: Implement rfo-config crate: load config from $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/rfo/config.toml, resolve XDG paths, validate config, write default config, generate JSON schema via schemars. Config sections: core, github, git, jobs, mcp, review, providers.claude, providers.codex, safety.
close_reason: XDG config paths + schema + validation implemented; 16 tests pass; cargo deny clean

title: rfo-28: GitHub issue/PR query
description: Implement GitHub issue and PR listing via octocrab: fetch open issues, open PRs, labels, CI status. Used by inbox and health. Cache results in context_cache table.

title: rfo-45: Policy parser
description: Implement policy YAML parser: required_files, github_labels, and extensible policy rules. Load from $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/rfo/policies.yaml. Validate schema.

title: rfo-20: Run timeline
description: Implement rfo run timeline (run-id): show what happened during a run, step by step, with timestamps and structured data. rfo run list and rfo run show for listing and single-run detail. New additive feature (no ru equivalent).

title: rfo-33: Context pack
description: Implement rfo context [repo] [--issue N] [--pr N]: produce context pack with local git status, GitHub issue/PR metadata, CI status, recent failures, quality gates, important files, risk notes, suggested commands. Output text/JSON. Cache in SQLite.

title: rfo-51: Docs
description: Write docs: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/CLI.md, docs/MCP.md, docs/SAFETY.md, docs/RU_PARITY.md. Cover install, config, safety, MCP, conflict handling, troubleshooting. Every command documented with examples.

title: rfo-12: init/add/remove/list/import
description: Implement core repo management commands: rfo init (initialize config + SQLite), rfo add (parse spec, lookup GitHub, insert), rfo remove, rfo list, rfo import (bulk import). These are the foundational CRUD operations.

title: rfo-41: Sweep commit
description: Implement rfo sweep commit: scan denylisted paths before staging, secret scan before commit, quality gates before push. No silent mutation. Records run.

title: rfo-27: Conflict plan/mark-resolved
description: Implement rfo conflict plan and rfo conflict mark-resolved. conflict plan creates a resolution plan (optionally AI-assisted). mark-resolved verifies no unmerged entries, no conflict markers, quality gates pass. Never silently auto-resolve. Apply requires confirmation.

title: add-1: Multi-repo targeting
description: Add --repos / --filter / --all flags to rfo sweep agent and rfo train run. Target by pattern (owner/*), filter (tag:needs-fmt, health:<50), or --all. Rules: --all requires --dry-run on first use or interactive confirmation. Concurrency bounded (default 4).

title: rfo-26: Conflict list/explain/abort
description: Implement rfo conflict list (show all repos in conflict), rfo conflict explain <repo> (show conflicted files, ours/theirs status, safe options), rfo conflict abort <repo> (safely abort merge/rebase/cherry-pick/revert). Detect MERGE_HEAD, REBASE_HEAD, CHERRY_PICK_HEAD, REVERT_HEAD, unmerged index entries, conflict markers.

title: rfo-5: Output modes
description: Implement rfo-output crate: text, JSON, NDJSON, TOON renderers. JSON must be schema-backed. NDJSON is append-friendly. TOON retained for ru compatibility. Text is concise and human-readable. This is used by every command that produces output.
close_reason: Output modes implemented: text, JSON, NDJSON, TOON; 18 tests pass; clippy/fmt clean

title: add-5: Auto-approve low
description: Add --auto-approve low flag to sweep agent and train run. Auto-approves LOW risk plans if gates pass; MEDIUM/HIGH still require confirmation. HIGH never auto-approved. Records auto-approve decisions in run history. Configurable via [agent.approval] auto_approve = none|low.

title: rfo-44: Train run
description: Implement rfo train run: execute planned fixers, create branch, run fixer, inspect diff, run gates, secret scan, commit, push, open PR, record run. Reject if too many files changed, denylisted path touched, secret scan fails, gates fail, suspicious deletion, patch too large.

title: rfo-36: MCP tools
description: Implement MCP tool endpoints with strict schema. Every tool has typed input/output. Mutating tools require confirmed: true. Every mutating tool creates a run record. Errors return structured JSON. AI must pass confirmed explicitly — no default.

title: rfo-32: CI log autopsy
description: Implement rfo ci autopsy [repo] [--pr number]: fetch failed jobs, step logs, extract error snippets, detect ecosystem, identify likely file/test, suggest next command, record failure class. No AI required for MVP. New additive feature.

title: rfo-38: Codex provider
description: Implement CodexProvider: invoke codex exec with configurable args. Parse output. Handle errors, timeouts, and provider unavailability. Provider is isolated behind the same trait as Claude.

title: rfo-40: Review plan/apply
description: Implement rfo review plan and rfo review apply: always plan first, show diff/risk/gates, AI edits use isolated branch/worktree, quality gates required, secret scan required. Safety-gated.

title: rfo-18: Parity E2E
description: Create E2E tests that verify rfo sync/status/prune produce equivalent output to ru for the same inputs. Use assert_cmd for CLI testing, tempfile for isolated repos, wiremock for GitHub API mocking. Document any accepted differences in docs/RU_PARITY.md.

title: rfo-10: GitHub auth
description: Implement rfo-github auth discovery: GITHUB_TOKEN env var, config token, gh CLI fallback, auto mode (try all in order). Never log or redact tokens. Handle rate limits with backoff. Auth is required for all GitHub API calls.
close_reason: GitHub auth discovery (env/gh/config-token/auto) implemented in commit 15bb3c5 with 7 unit tests. AuthToken redacts in Debug/Display. Closes rfo-10.

title: rfo-30: Inbox scoring
description: Implement rfo inbox: aggregate signals from GitHub PRs, issues, failed checks, local dirty/diverged repos, failed runs, policy violations. Priority classes: CRITICAL, HIGH, NORMAL, LOW, INFO. Show next action suggestion. New additive feature.

title: rfo-42: Sweep agent
description: Implement rfo sweep agent --plan: AI-driven sweep that creates a plan first, then applies through plan/apply. AI edits use isolated branch/worktree. Quality gates and secret scan required. Denylist enforced. Safety-gated AI automation path.

title: rfo-34: MCP stdio
description: Implement rfo-mcp: MCP server via rmcp with stdio transport. Tools: rfo.status, rfo.inbox, rfo.health, rfo.context, rfo.review_plan, rfo.job_status, rfo.run_timeline. Resources: rfo://repos, rfo://inbox, rfo://context/{owner}/{repo}, rfo://runs/{id}/timeline. Mutation tools create plans, never apply directly.

title: rfo-46: Policy check
description: Implement rfo policy check: check repos against loaded policies, report violations, suggest fixes. Check only — do not auto-apply GitHub settings. Read-only diagnostic.

title: rfo-52: v1 launch checklist
description: Complete v1 launch checklist: core commands work, SQLite is source of truth, RU_PARITY.md complete, parity tests pass, sync/status/prune stable, run timeline works, failure memory handles common errors, plan/apply/rollback exists, conflict resolver works, inbox useful, context packs useful, review/sweep gated, train handles deterministic fixes, release pipeline produces artifacts, docs complete.
